text.title: Market Research: TAM/SAM/SOM Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Market Research: TAM/SAM/SOM methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Every TAM/SAM/SOM artefact MUST include both a top-down number (industry-report-scaled) and a bottom-up number (price x reachable units).
rule.rationale: Single-source sizing is unauditable; triangulation surfaces hidden assumptions.

rule.statement: If top-down and bottom-up disagree by more than 30%, write an explicit reconciliation paragraph; do not just average them.
rule.rationale: A 30%+ delta signals a wrong assumption (geography, ICP, price band); averaging hides the bug.

rule.statement: Every TAM/SAM/SOM number MUST cite a URL + retrieval date.
rule.rationale: Industry reports change; without a date the number cannot be replicated.

rule.statement: SOM MUST carry a confidence tag (high/medium/low) with a one-line reason.
rule.rationale: Unhedged SOM in a pitch deck is a credibility liability.

rule.statement: TAM/SAM/SOM MUST be computed for an explicitly pinned geography list + ICP definition; refuse 'global' as a geography.
rule.rationale: Global TAM is meaningless for a product that ships in 3 countries.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec — decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
